lessons.example_en: I wash my hands before eating.
lessons.note: Attach **-기 전에** to a verb stem to say the action happens *before* the next one. A noun takes **전에** directly (수업 전에 = before class).

sentences.english: I wash my hands before eating.
I brush my teeth before sleeping.
I drink coffee before studying.
I check the weather before going out.
I exercise before breakfast.

lessons.example_en: I take a shower after exercising.
lessons.note: Attach **-ㄴ 후에** after a vowel stem and **-은 후에** after a consonant stem. **-(으)ㄴ 다음에** means the same thing.

sentences.english: I take a shower after exercising.
I drink coffee after eating.
I rest after class ends.
I read a book after doing my homework.
I brush my teeth after eating.

lessons.meaning: when / at the time of (doing something)
lessons.example_en: When I go to Korea, I take a plane.
lessons.note: Attach **-ㄹ 때** after a vowel stem and **-을 때** after a consonant stem. Use **-았/었을 때** for a completed past moment (어렸을 때 = when I was young).

sentences.english: When I go to Korea, I take a plane.
When I'm tired, I drink coffee.
When I have time, I read books.
When I eat, I watch TV.
When the weather is nice, I take a walk.

lessons.example_en: I study while listening to music.
lessons.note: Attach **-면서** after a vowel stem and **-으면서** after a consonant stem to show one subject doing two actions at the same time.

sentences.english: I study while listening to music.
I eat while watching TV.
I drink coffee while reading a book.
I sing while taking a walk.
I talk with a friend while drinking coffee.

lessons.example_en: I have been to Jeju Island.
lessons.note: Attach **-ㄴ 적이 있다** after a vowel stem and **-은 적이 있다** after a consonant stem to say you have the experience of doing it. Negate with **-(으)ㄴ 적이 없다** (have never done).

sentences.english: I have been to Jeju Island.
I have eaten Korean food.
I have met a famous person.
I have learned Korean.
I have never read this book.

lessons.example: 한국어를 배운 지 일 년 됐어요.
lessons.example_en: It has been one year since I started learning Korean.
lessons.note: Attach **-ㄴ 지** after a vowel stem and **-은 지** after a consonant stem, then state how much time has passed with **됐어요** or **지났어요**.

sentences.english: It has been one year since I started learning Korean.
sentences.korean: 한국어를 배운 지 일 년 됐어요.
accepted: 한국어를 배운 지 일 년 됐어요.
한국어를 배운 지 일 년 됐습니다.
한국어를 배운 지 일 년이 됐어요.
한국어를 배운 지 일 년 지났어요.

sentences.english: It has been two hours since I ate.
sentences.korean: 밥을 먹은 지 두 시간 됐어요.
accepted: 밥을 먹은 지 두 시간 됐어요.
밥을 먹은 지 두 시간 됐습니다.
밥을 먹은 지 두 시간이 됐어요.
밥을 먹은 지 두 시간 지났어요.

sentences.english: It has been three months since I came to Korea.
sentences.korean: 한국에 온 지 세 달 됐어요.
accepted: 한국에 온 지 세 달 됐어요.
한국에 온 지 세 달 됐습니다.
한국에 온 지 세 달이 됐어요.
한국에 온 지 삼 개월 됐어요.

sentences.english: It has been a week since I met my friend.
It has been a long time since I read a book.
